title: Fastly Backend
description: A backend represents an origin server that a Fastly service routes requests to for content that is not cached at the edge. Backends define the address, port, SSL configuration, health checks, and load balancing parameters for origin connections.

name.description: The name of the backend, used as a unique identifier within the service version.

address.description: The hostname or IPv4 address of the backend origin server.

port.description: The port number on the backend origin server.

use_ssl.description: Whether to use SSL/TLS to connect to the backend.

ssl_hostname.description: The hostname to verify for SSL certificate validation.

ssl_cert_hostname.description: The hostname to use when checking the backend SSL certificate.

ssl_sni_hostname.description: The SNI hostname to use for the backend TLS connection.

shield.description: The data center identifier to use as a shield POP for this backend.

weight.description: The weight assigned to this backend for load balancing purposes.

connect_timeout.description: Maximum duration in milliseconds to wait for a connection to the backend.

first_byte_timeout.description: Maximum duration in milliseconds to wait for the first byte from the backend.

between_bytes_timeout.description: Maximum duration in milliseconds to wait between bytes received from the backend.

max_conn.description: Maximum number of simultaneous connections to the backend.

healthcheck.description: The name of the healthcheck to use with the backend.

auto_loadbalance.description: Whether to auto-load-balance this backend with others that share the same healthcheck.

request_condition.description: The name of a condition to apply to route requests to this backend.

comment.description: A freeform descriptive note about the backend.
